name: Branch Protection Setup
description: Configure branch protection rules to enforce code review, status checks, and merge requirements on important branches.

steps.title: View current branch protection rules
steps.description: Check what protection rules are currently set on a branch.

steps.title: Enable required pull request reviews
steps.description: Require at least one approval before merging to main.
steps.warning: This applies to everyone including admins when enforce_admins is true.

steps.title: Require status checks to pass
steps.description: Ensure CI checks pass before a PR can be merged.
steps.command: gh api -X PUT repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/main/protection/required_status_checks -f strict=true -f contexts[]='ci/test' -f contexts[]='ci/lint'
steps.tip: Set strict=true to require the branch to be up-to-date with the base branch before merging.

steps.title: View rulesets (newer alternative)
steps.description: List repository rulesets which provide more flexible branch protection.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Branch Protection Setup
set -euo pipefail

REPO=${1:?"Usage: $0 <owner/repo>"}
BRANCH=${2:-"main"}

echo "=== Current Protection for $BRANCH ==="
gh api "repos/$REPO/branches/$BRANCH/protection" 2>/dev/null | jq '{
  required_reviews: .required_pull_request_reviews.required_approving_review_count,
  dismiss_stale_reviews: .required_pull_request_reviews.dismiss_stale_reviews,
  status_checks: .required_status_checks.contexts,
  enforce_admins: .enforce_admins.enabled,
  linear_history: .required_linear_history.enabled
}' || echo "No branch protection configured"

echo ""
echo "=== Rulesets ==="
gh api "repos/$REPO/rulesets" --jq '.[] | .name + " (" + .enforcement + ")"' 2>/dev/null || echo "No rulesets configured"

echo ""
echo "=== Protection Commands ==="
echo "View:   gh api repos/$REPO/branches/$BRANCH/protection"
echo "Delete: gh api -X DELETE repos/$REPO/branches/$BRANCH/protection"

faq.question: What is the difference between branch protection and rulesets?
faq.answer: Rulesets are the newer, more flexible system. They support multiple rule targets, organization-level rules, and bypass lists. Branch protection rules are per-branch and simpler to configure.

faq.question: Can I protect branches matching a pattern?
faq.answer: With branch protection rules, you can use wildcard patterns like 'release/*'. With rulesets, you get even more flexible ref name patterns.
